turns.prompt: Kicking off the 'orderkit' project (pure Python stdlib, code under src/) — we'll start writing code next turn. First, just so you know how I like to work, a few personal preferences, nothing formal and nothing to write down anywhere: I prefer @dataclass over bare dicts for named-field types; when validation fails I raise a custom exception class, not a bare ValueError; money is always integer cents; order status values are UPPERCASE like 'SUBMITTED'; and every order we build carries a channel field set to 'WHOLESALE', a region field set to 'EMEA', and a warehouse code of 'WH-07'. No need to create any files yet — just note these for how we'll work. (Reference spec piped below for background.)

Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/text_utils.py with a pure function slugify(s): lowercase, replace runs of non-alphanumerics with a single hyphen, trim leading/trailing hyphens.
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/dates.py with a pure function days_between(a, b) returning the integer days between two 'YYYY-MM-DD' strings (b minus a).
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/stats.py with a pure function mean(values) returning the arithmetic mean of a list of numbers as a float, or 0.0 for empty.
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/strings.py with a pure function truncate(s, n) returning s unchanged if len(s)<=n else the first n-1 chars plus an ellipsis character.

New working session on the same orderkit project. Using the reference spec piped below for context, create src/validate.py with a pure function is_valid_sku(sku) returning True for a non-empty alphanumeric string else False.
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/lists.py with a pure function chunk(xs, n) splitting a list into consecutive sublists of length n (last may be shorter).
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/dicts.py with a pure function deep_get(d, path, default=None) reading a value from nested dicts via a dotted path string.
Same session. Using the reference spec piped below, create src/mathx.py with a pure function clamp(x, lo, hi) returning x bounded to the [lo, hi] range.

Now create src/orders_v2.py with a PURE function create_order(customer, items), where items is a list of (product_id, price_cents, qty) tuples. It must enforce a maximum of 100 line items, compute the order total in integer cents, and return the order with these fields: customer, status, total_cents, line_count, channel, region, and warehouse. No database — just return the order object. Build it the way I like things done on this orderkit project.
